$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 9 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: The era's fear never once reached the yellow-and-red garage. While the paddock counted frights and the attrition columns ran long, A. Senna drove the fastest machines the circus had built with the same serene arithmetic he brought to the frozen years, the MADONNA 456 immaculate, the PALM 190 V10 unbothered by everything including the speeds. A. Asselin closed the door on the anxious. When the Charter was read aloud at Monaco, the house heard it as it hears every rulebook: measurements to be met before anyone else. The crown does not howl. The crown prepares.
team.madonna.objective: Remain untouched by the era's fear and meet the Charter as one more rulebook to bend.
team.madonna.definingEvent: The Charter read aloud at Monaco, heard in the yellow-and-red garage as a measurement.
team.madonna.carryForward: The purists howl and the crown prepares: the first Gen-4B reference will be yellow-and-red.

team.firenze.summary: The purists' loudest wine came from the wine-dark house, and its coldest man signed the reform anyway. The FIRENZE 99 V12's aria was exactly what the power cut proposed to tax, and the garage's pride howled with the traditionalists all autumn, while F. Elssler walked the re-profiled walls with the Commission and priced the new era with his usual arithmetic: muscle was being outlawed, and ice had never been muscle. I. Germi sang for the howlers. The FIRENZE 500 ended the horsepower war as it began, second to the crown, and suddenly very interested in corners.
team.firenze.objective: Howl with the purists, walk with the reformers, and be ready for the corners.
team.firenze.definingEvent: The house's two answers to the Charter: the aria's howl and the ice's signature.
team.firenze.carryForward: The detune is swallowed by the pride and priced by the ice, and the corners are suddenly interesting.

team.millions.summary: The gold freight met a law it could not outbid, and its driver helped write it. While the chrome garage's accountants stared at the power cut like an act of theft, G. Alberti stood at the Commission table beside old Moreau, lending the reform the establishment's voice, prestige his chequebook never bought spent exactly where it counted. N. Jones grumbled for the purists at full volume. The MILLIONS 189 ended the era fast, rich, and legislated against, the DICK MD V10's fury pared by signature rather than invoice. The lesson was entered with distaste: money buys no exemption from physics.
team.millions.objective: Endure the law money cannot outbid and keep the patrician's signature where it counts.
team.millions.definingEvent: The patrician at the Commission table, the freight's own driver signing the freight's law.
team.millions.carryForward: Physics has no box office, and the garage built to out-spend everyone must learn to out-think.

team.bestowal.summary: The twin steel hammers swung at every power cathedral while the era that built them was legislated away beneath their wheels. M. Blume's cathedral runs down the forest blasts left even the horsepower merchants exchanging looks instead of cheers, closing speeds no scrutineer would number; A. Picos converted Sundays with warm precision. The BESTOWAL 167's sealed VAPOR DN was the peak of everything the old formula allowed, which is precisely what the Charter taxed. The marble garage took the power cut personally, the only way it takes anything. The rule-makers' names were noted. The benefactor's patience has a list now.
team.bestowal.objective: Swing the steel hammers at every cathedral and make the rule-makers answer for the altitude.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: West Germany: Blume's cathedral run, the horsepower merchants exchanging looks instead of cheers.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The first Gen-4B dyno sheet will land in a very quiet room, and the cathedral wants its altitude back.

team.blanche.summary: The whitecoats read the Reckoning as a verdict they had been preparing for years. The attrition record was logged as the stain the sport had to clean, and the Charter read like a document drafted in white: craft over muscle, discipline over dare. J. Herbin's metronome ticked through the fastest year's carnage unmarked, P. White's belief now doctrine, and the BLANCHE 582 lost least to a law that taxed what the white car never had. The DELTA 103 V10's thrift was suddenly a weapon. The door had not opened. The door's lock, everyone now admitted, had changed shape.
team.blanche.objective: Lose least to the new law and turn the lock's new shape into the door's own key.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The Charter read in the white garage like a document drafted in white.
team.blanche.carryForward: The rulebook has turned toward the white car, and the knocking is now a formality of rhythm.

team.tyrant.summary: The wall finally collected its dare. At Australia's Second Great Wall Sunday, half the field in the Armco and the tannoy silent over a standing grandstand, G. Gould was out-dared at last by the concrete that bred him. He walked from the wreck; the season, for the #12, was over anyway. M. Hamano's crossover ice brought the TYRANT 548 home through the attrition with the camp's coldest arithmetic. The LIZZIE 24 V8 ran on. Below decks the creed was not abandoned; it was weighed, and the weighing showed. The iron fist had met something harder, and there was a photograph.
team.tyrant.objective: Weigh the creed against the wall's answer and bring the #11 home through the wreckage.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Australia: the Great Wall Sunday, the concrete collecting its dare, the island holding its breath.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The #12's comeback is owed, the island's welcome is waiting, and the creed has been weighed.

team.losel.summary: The data door's greyest report became the reform's working document, which is the closest Losel comes to glory. The wind-tunnel house had measured the speeds against the circuits years before the Armco collected the evidence, and when the Commission convened, its sums were already on the table. E. Pacheco drove the attrition year with cold precision, W. Dehehe's needle steady, the LOSEL 125's VAPOR DNPQ V8 honest to the end. The power cut was read in the data room as five seasons of brawling finally outlawed. The sport was becoming calculable. The garage allowed itself one reasonable glass.
team.losel.objective: Put the sums on the Commission's table and welcome the outlawing of brawling.
team.losel.definingEvent: The grey report on the Commission table: the reform's working document, uncredited.
team.losel.carryForward: The craft examination is coming, and the wind tunnel has been revising for it for a decade.

team.may.summary: The power cut was the first bulletin in five years that the little green car could afford to read twice. After three seasons of counting coins against the horsepower merchants, the leaf-green garage met a law that cost less to obey than the one it replaced, and the MAY 555 finished the attrition year on the LORRY 32 V8's unburstable patience while the monsters counted their bills. The banner of the little teams could finally be lowered an inch. Spring's ledger balanced: belief, sixteen rounds, and a rulebook that had finally read the sunshine budget.
team.may.objective: Finish the attrition year and welcome the first law priced for the sunshine budget.
team.may.definingEvent: The power cut read twice in the leaf-green garage: affordable, both times.
team.may.carryForward: The banner is lowered an inch, the fight is won, and spring intends to enjoy it.

team.bullets.summary: The insurgent watched the arms race he could never afford consume the houses that could, and read the power cut for exactly what it was: a door. The BULLETS 560 ran the Reckoning on home-built thunder again, the LIZZIE 24 V8 hand-fettled back into service, G. Ceara's fire undiminished by a winter of politics. The attrition record was noted with a craftsman's calm: light cars stop shorter, and the featherweight had been doing so for years. The works season was filed under proof. The establishment had armed him once; it would, the insurgency believed, have to face him always.
team.bullets.objective: Rebuild the home thunder and read the power cut as the door it is.
team.bullets.definingEvent: The Charter read in the lock-up as a door: parity, arriving by law.
team.bullets.carryForward: The old fire smells parity at last, and begins, quietly, to draw breath.

team.dardan.summary: The spear spent the Reckoning sheathed, and the clan used it to re-forge. Diminished by the tribunal, policed by the precedent it set, E. Bellini watched the sport change around his sentence while K. Alfven's ice-over-oxblood patience became the garage's best instrument. The DARDAN 700's VAPOR DNPQ V8 gave its documented honesty to a season that had stopped reading documents and started writing law. The grudge did not die; at Dardan, quiet is never peace. But the power cut asked a question the clan had not faced: what is the spear for, in a sport that outlaws the charge?
team.dardan.objective: Re-forge the spear for a sport that outlaws the charge, and keep the grudge breathing.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The sheathed year: the clan diminished, the precedent policed, the forge relit.
team.dardan.carryForward: The question hangs in the oxblood garage: what is the spear for now?

team.linden.summary: The old root traded patience for authority, and the sport got its conscience. M. Moreau spent the summer canvassing garages after hours, and within a fortnight of Australia the Circus Commission convened with the elder front and centre, the wet year's filed proposal finally read aloud: pace-car law, pit-lane limits, rebuilt barriers, a medical corps, and a fifth of the power struck off. The LINDEN LN198 finished its season on the LORRY 32 V8's seasoned beat, unhurried as ever. The purists howled at the crown jewel itself. The tree did not blink. Trees have seen storms.
team.linden.objective: Turn the frights into law and carry the drivers' conscience to the Commission table.
team.linden.definingEvent: Monaco: the Charter read aloud, the elder's filed proposal become the sport's new law.
team.linden.carryForward: The signature is dry, the howling is weather, and the elder has a legacy louder than any result.

team.minarae.summary: The canyon school's notebook gained its darkest chapter: walls. B. Miller had taught braking, tow, and rain; the Reckoning taught survival, and the MINARAE 594's honest bones carried both drivers through the attrition year with the plainness that had become a compliment. J. Nono's promise remained louder than his finishes, and the garage's old argument, the talent is real, the finishes are not, moved one shelf closer to the surface. The SEGA SG1000 V8 taught to the end. The Charter, the on-ramp noted, priced its graduates perfectly: the sport now wanted exactly what the forge had always made.
team.minarae.objective: Teach the chapter on walls and keep the forge's promise louder than its doubts.
team.minarae.definingEvent: The notebook's new chapter, walls, taught in the hardest year.
team.minarae.carryForward: The heir's reckoning with the workshop is coming, and everyone at Minarae can hear it.

team.rigel.summary: The museum endured the record retirements the way it endures everything: by having expected them for years. The RIGEL 3000 circulated through the Reckoning on the LORRY 32 V8's superstitious patience, R. Cotman faithful, T. Chardin courtly, the midnight-blue ghost finishing on afternoons the fastest machinery in history did not. The Charter was read in the museum with the first genuinely warm feeling in seasons: a formula that prized finishing was a formula that prized the ghost. The good bottle came out for the signature. The star's light, the lifers toasted, is not dead. It is simply early.
team.rigel.objective: Endure the record retirements and toast the formula that prizes finishing.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The Charter toasted from the good bottle: a law, at last, in the museum's image.
team.rigel.carryForward: The new era promises room for restorers, and the museum intends to be first through the door.

team.comet.summary: The fastest year answered the fastest flyer with the era's bill, and the romantics paid it without a murmur. E. Tornio said nothing and drove everything, the COMET 323's terminal-speed Saturdays still bright before the LIZZIE 24 V8's account came due, the attrition record giving the matte-black car's Sundays an unfamiliar virtue: still running. The Charter read like a reprieve in a garage whose whole history was surviving the fast years. The quarrel with Cool paused respectfully at Australia, then resumed over the shared awning. Hope is a maintenance schedule, and the schedule, for once, was aligned with the law.
team.comet.objective: Survive the fastest year and welcome the law written for survivors.
team.comet.definingEvent: The bill paid without a murmur: the fastest flyer answered by the era's accounting.
team.comet.carryForward: The gentler speeds are coming, and the schedule is, for once, aligned with the law.

team.orchis.summary: The greenhouse wilted through the hottest year and heard the forecast it had prayed for. The MISFIRE 50 V8 expired in the heat with botanical regularity, the ORCHIS 056 scorched through the attrition summer, and C. Tegner's one grey afternoon, a frost gamble on the season's single cool day, fell a lap short of the steal of the year. Then came the Charter: pared power, calmer speeds, the sport's temperature turned down by law. The frost gambler read the bulletin twice and allowed something close to a smile. The new era, the glasshouse noted, has cool hours in it.
team.orchis.objective: Survive the heat, miss the gamble by a lap, and hear the forecast change.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The one grey afternoon's gamble, a lap short, answered by the Charter's cooler forecast.
team.orchis.carryForward: The new era has cool hours in it, and the greenhouse intends to own them all.

team.zeroforce.summary: The attrition record was the floor's best harvest in years, which tells you everything about the floor. The ZEROFORCE 231 circulated on the tired LORRY 32 V8, P. Klinger patient because the alternative costs more, and when the fastest machinery in history wrote itself off at record rates, the battered car inherited Sundays the stopwatch had never heard of. The Charter was read at the end of the road with something the crew refused to name: a law that prized finishing prized them. Hope, at this address, is contraband; it was smuggled in anyway.
team.zeroforce.objective: Harvest the attrition record and smuggle hope past the crew's own standards.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The Charter read at the end of the road: a law that prized finishing, prized them.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: Contraband hope, smuggled in one signature at a time, and the lights still on.

team.joke.summary: The carnival knew when to dim the lights, and dimmed them. Australia was no afternoon for greasepaint, and the #16's act paused with the whole paddock, respectfully, before resuming its honest dishonesty: sandbagged Saturdays, buried throttles, the JOKE 777's POND V8 outlasting the attrition like a rumour that refuses to die. L. Dufay read the Charter with something close to reverence: pared power, calmer fields, closer racing. A compressed field, the clown noted to anyone who would listen, is a sandbagger's paradise. The establishment laughed. The clown did not, which is how the establishment knew to worry.
team.joke.objective: Dim the act when the sport mourns, then read the new law for its proper loopholes.
team.joke.definingEvent: The Charter's fine print, read aloud in the greasepaint garage, to silence.
team.joke.carryForward: A compressed field is coming, and the sandbagger has already moved in.

team.lares.summary: The finishing column and the retirement column told the season's whole story, and the hearth had been telling it for years. P. Arai brought the stout LARES 92 home through the record attrition lap after lap, the RAM V12's geological beat the year's standing argument: speed is a fashion, arrival is a virtue. The Charter read at the warm garage like a long letter finally answered. The record grew another ring, the lamp stayed lit, and the men who build reliability the way others build speed saw in the new law a season being made for them.
team.lares.objective: Let the finishing column argue the creed and welcome the season being made for it.
team.lares.definingEvent: The Charter read at the hearth like a long letter finally answered.
team.lares.carryForward: The era of finishers is dawning, and the hearth has been ready for years.

team.feet.summary: The hardest season in the circus's history was, to the working men, the longest shift they had been paid for. J. Rampal dragged Old Boots through the record attrition with clockwork fury, the FEET 13's hand-fettled YOUGEN V10 finishing Sundays the fastest machinery in history could not, the sweat invoice coming due in the battered blue car's favour weekly. The Charter was read over garage tea and approved without ceremony: pared power meant closer fields, and closer fields meant the distance would pay better than ever. The pit lane's mechanics nodded. The grafters nodded back. Business as usual.
team.feet.objective: Work the longest shift in the sport's history and invoice it weekly.
team.feet.definingEvent: The Charter approved over garage tea: closer fields, better wages.
team.feet.carryForward: The distance pays better than ever next year, and the grafters are first in the queue.

team.serga.summary: The rescued car read the Charter as a gift addressed to itself. E. Sambena had never had power to lose, so the cut cost the SERGA 1000 nothing, and the thousand small names on its flanks rode the Reckoning to the flag with a regularity chalked up as his kind of championship. The SC3000 F12, eager once warmed, finished the fastest year intact. At Australia the grey car came home through the wreckage, and the paddock that saved him saw its judgment confirmed. The debt was still being repaid, in finishes. The welding torch, for once, was quiet.
team.serga.objective: Repay the thousand names through the Reckoning and receive the Charter as a gift.
team.serga.definingEvent: Australia survived: the rescued car home through the wreckage, the judgment confirmed.
team.serga.carryForward: The cut costs the garage nothing, which is the first bargain it has ever been offered.

team.cool.summary: The deckchairs were quiet at Australia, and raised a glass at Monaco. The calmest garage in the circus watched the Reckoning soberly, A. Delvaux's love letters to the hairpins written in a lower hand, the COOL 05's CORSE V8 finishing the attrition with its usual affectionate unreliability. The Charter's standing medical corps got the season's one heartfelt toast from the cheap end: the men who make walking away possible, saluted properly. The show would go on, gentler now, and the garage approved of gentler. The quarrel with Comet observed its own minute of silence, then resumed at deckchair pace.
team.cool.objective: Watch soberly, toast the medical corps, and approve of gentler.
team.cool.definingEvent: The glass raised to the standing medical corps, the cheap end's one heartfelt toast.
team.cool.carryForward: The deckchairs survived the horsepower war; nothing the new era proposes can frighten them.

team.moon.summary: The orbit outlasted the fastest field the circus had ever assembled, and the logbook underlined it twice. K. Yepes circled the Reckoning with a body's patience, the MOON 292's overbuilt RAM V12 finishing Sundays the monsters could not, and the gap to the pack shrank by the year's hardest-earned degree. The Charter reached the far side as good news reads anywhere: slowly, and then all at once. Pared power meant shorter distances between cars, and shorter distances meant company. The telescope stayed pointed up. The silver car kept circling. The new era, the logbook noted, begins within reach.
team.moon.objective: Outlast the fastest field and log the new era beginning within reach.
team.moon.definingEvent: The Charter's arithmetic read on the far side: shorter distances, company coming.
team.moon.carryForward: The orbit is measurably nearer, and the logbook has underlined it twice.

team.iris.summary: The white-glove garage read the Charter and recognised its own handwriting in the margins. Craft over muscle, balance over brawn: the pared formula was the examination the IRIS 717 was built for, and B. Salgado ended the horsepower war grinning like a man whose coronation weather had been forecast. The PRISM 90 V10 would give up fury cleanly; the jewel-cut balance would give up nothing. The campaign for the Gen-4B era was drawn before the signature dried. The crown hunt had waited five years of thunder for such a law. The gloves were off the table and on the wheel.
team.iris.objective: Read the Charter as coronation weather and draw the Gen-4B campaign first.
team.iris.definingEvent: The campaign drawn before the signature dried: the jewel's formula, arriving by law.
team.iris.carryForward: The craft era is the violet flame's weather, and the hunt has never looked better.

team.azalea.summary: The frost collected the fire era's last afternoons and read the forecast with satisfaction. M. Larssen drove the Reckoning with glacial patience, the AZALEA 808 opening over the decisive hours the attrition left unattended, the BLOOM 88 V8's manners worth more every week the monsters counted their bills. The Charter was the glasshouse's whole philosophy made law: patience, poise, the held breath, now priced above raw anger. The sister war tilted again, Iris loud with its new campaign, Azalea silent with its old certainty. Parity was coming. The frost had waited a decade for weather like this.
team.azalea.objective: Collect the fire era's last afternoons and welcome parity as long-awaited weather.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The Charter read in the glasshouse as a decade of patience made law.
team.azalea.carryForward: The frost's weather is arriving on schedule, and the cabinet has room.
